notable.notable_blurb: Short-story writer published in Atlantic Monthly

career.notes: Published short stories and articles in Atlantic Monthly on themes including untrained nurses, the waterfront, flags, schools, and Quaker teachers.

cross_connections.display_label: Lifelong partner; lived together in Asheville NC and Wilkes-Barre PA

narrative_blocks.body: She published 'A North-West End' in Atlantic Monthly in 1909 and saw another story included in the Best Short Stories of 1915. Her published work focused on untrained nurses, the waterfront, flags, schools, and Quaker teachers — themes drawn from her circles in Wilkes-Barre and the rural South.

narrative_blocks.header: Life partner of Elizabeth Dwight Packard
narrative_blocks.body: By 1900 she and Elizabeth Dwight Packard (H04333) were living together as 'head' and 'partner' in the federal census taken at Buncombe County, North Carolina. After Elizabeth's death in 1915 Lucy was sole executor and inherited most of her estate; her own obituary in 1940 insisted Wilkes-Barre had always been her home.
